keyPoints: Approximately 2.5% of the Earth's surface is freshwater, but 99.9% of it is frozen in glaciers and polar ice caps.
Most of the world's water is undrinkable and inedible, and the majority of the world's population lacks access to clean drinking water.
Quality aspects: pH levels, turbidity, bacterial contamination, and nutrient pollution can all impact water quality, making it unsafe for consumption and other uses.
